q: Allelopathy is a form of interspecific competition in which:
options: One species physically excludes another from feeding sites through aggressive behavior
A plant or microorganism releases chemical compounds into the environment that inhibit the germination or growth of neighboring species
One species evolves to mimic another's warning coloration to avoid predation
A fungus produces toxins that kill its host plant so it can decompose the organic matter
explanation: Allelopathic chemicals (e.g., juglone from black walnut trees, antibiotics from bacteria) are released into soil or water to reduce competition from other plants or microbes.
